properties.remark: A PILOT REPORTED A LARGE DUST STORM EXTENDING TO 18000 FT MSL IN THE NORTHERN PORTION OF THE OWENS VALLEY.
A PILOT REPORTED A LARGE DUST STORM EXTENDING TO 18000 FT MSL IN THE NORTHERN PORTION OF THE OWENS VALLEY.

BLOWING DUST AND VISIBILITY REDUCE TO NEAR ZERO.
BLOWING DUST AND VISIBILITY REDUCED TO NEAR ZERO.

*** 2 INJ *** DUST STORM CONDITIONS CONTRIBUTED TO A PILEUP ON INTERSTATE 40 INVOLVING APPROXIMATELY 10 BIG RIGS. THE HIGHWAY WAS STILL CLOSED AS OF MIDNIGHT. THERE WERE AT LEAST TWO REPORTED INJURIES.

LAKE MEAD DISPATCH REPORTED BOATS...INCLUDING AT LEAST ONE HOUSE BOAT...WERE BEACHED DUE TO THE STRONG OUTFLOW WINDS FROM THUNDERSTORMS WELL TO THE SOUTHEAST IN ARIZONA. THE EXTENT OF ANY DAMAGE IS CURRENTLY UNKNOWN. TIME ESTMATED BASED OFF OBSERVATIONS.

PARK RANGER REPORTED THAT SHERRIFFS OFFICE CLOSED OFF PARTS OF HIGHWAY 178 NEAR SHOSHONE DUE TO FLOODING. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.

CALIFORNIA HIGHWAY PATROL IS REPORTING FLOODING ON HIGHWAY 127 NORTH OF INTERSTATE 15. THERE ARE REPORTS OF VEHICLES STUCK. CALTRANS HAS ALSO REPORTED THAT THEY WILL BE SHUTTING DOWN HIGHWAY 127 FROM INTERSTATE 15 TO THE INYO COUNTY LINE DUE TO SEVERAL AREAS OF FLOODING.

SPOTTER REPORTS DEBRIS OVER ROADWAY NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF AMBOY RD AND THOMPSON ROAD. A HOME IN THE AREA ALSO HAS 4 TO 6 INCHES OF STANDING WATER IN THE YARD OVER 24 HOURS AFTER THE STORM. TIME OF REPORT ESTEMATED FROM RADAR.

US 95 FLOODED AND IMPASSABLE FOR A 20 FOOT SECTION OF ROAD WITH ROCKS AND DEBRIS
A TRAINED SPOTTER IN BISHOP REPORTED 6-8 INCH TREE BRANCHES BLOWN DOWN AND BLOCKING HALF OF HANBY AVE IN BISHOP. WINDS AT THE BISHOP ASOS WERE GUSTING TO 45 MPH AT THE TIME.

CLARK COUNTY FLOOD CONTROL DISTRICT REPORTED THAT A DEBRIS FLOW KNOCKED THE GATE TO THE GRIFFITH PEAK TRAILHEAD OFF ITS HINGES. AT LEAST 200 FEET OF DEBRIS CROSSED HARRIS SPRINGS ROAD MAKING IT IMPASSIBLE. THE EVENT TIME IS AN ESTIMATE.

THUNDERSTORM WINDS SHEARED 4 TRANSMISSION POLES SOUTH OF GRIFFITH ROAD. THIS KNOCKED OUT POWER TO ALL OF GOLDEN VALLEY AND SOUTH DOWNTOWN KINGMAN FOR ABOUT 2 HOURS AND IN CHLORIDE, DOLAN SPRINGS, MEADVIEW AND WHITE HILLS UNTIL 6 AM MONDAY, JULY 7TH.
MUD WASHED ONTO BULLHEAD PARKWAY NEAR MILE MARKER 6. THE EVENT TIME IS AN ESTIMATE.
4 TO 6 INCHES OF WATER AN MUD FLOWING ACROSS SIDE ROAD OFF BLUE DIAMOND ROAD. REPORTED VIA FACEBOOK WITH PHOTO. REPORT TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.

FLASH FLOODING WAS REPORTED ON HIGHWAY 62 BETWEEN MT. STASTA AVE TO LEAR STREET. AS OF 509 PM CALTRANS HAS SCRAPPED THE ROAD AND TRAFFIC IS FLOWING SMOOTHLY.

A PICTURE OF A HABOOB MOVING THROUGH DOLAN SPRINGS WAS POSTED ON FACEBOOK. VISIBILITY WAS ESTIMATED TO BE LESS THAN ONE HALF MILE.
SPOTTER CALLED TO REPORT THAT VISIBILITY IN CHLORIDE WAS 600 FEET OR LESS.

*** 4 INJ *** BLOWING DUST RESULTED IN REDUCED VISIBILITY ALONG HIGHWAY 93 TRIGGERING A VEHICLE ROLLOVER NEAR MILE MARKER 53. FOUR PEOPLE IN THE VEHICLE WERE TREATED ON SCENE WITH ONE OF THE FOUR TRANSPORTED FOR MEDICAL CARE.

CLARK COUNTY FIRE AND CITY OF LAS VEGAS FIRE HAVE CONDUCTED WELLNESS CHECKS ON RAINBOW SUBDIVISION RESIDENTS AND HOMES. ALL RESIDENTS REPORTED SAFE AND SHELTERING IN PLACE. SIX HOMES WERE DEEMED DAMAGED, WITH TWO HAVING SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE.
RAINBOW CANYON ACCESS ROAD WAS DAMAGED AND UTILITY LINES WERE DAMAGED. 112 CUSTOMERS HAD NO WATER DUE TO A PIPE THAT WAS DAMAGED.
RUNOFF AND DEBRIS 5 FEET DEEP RUNNING DOWN RAINBOW CANYON ROAD.
CALIFORNIA HIGHWAY PATROL REPORTED PORTIONS OF THE PAVEMENT ON I-15 AT HALLORAN SUMMIT ROAD EXIT WASHED AWAY.
GRAVEL WASHED ONTO THE ACCESS ROAD TO WILLOW BEACH. THE NATIONAL PARK SERVICE CLOSED THE ROAD AS A RESULT.
CALIFORNIA HIGHWAY PATROL REPORTED WATER AND MUD COVERED AS MUCH AS A 30 FOOT SWATH OF NIPTON ROAD. CREWS WERE BEING DISPATCHED TO CLEAR THE ROAD.
LINCOLN COUNTY SO REPORTED STATE ROUTE 322 FROM MILE MARKER 5 TO THE ROSE VALLEY TURNOFF WAS CLOSED DUE TO FLOODING. A PORTION OF THE ROADWAY WAS WASHED OUT.
THUNDERSTORM WINDS KNOCKED TREES DOWN ONTO POWER LINES IN PEACH SPRINGS.
AS MUCH AS 8 TO 10 INCHES OF FLOWING WATER, MUD AND ROCKS WASHED ACROSS LOW SPOTS ON PAVED ROADS IN PEACH SPRINGS.

BLOWING DUST OFF A DRY LAKE BED NEAR NEWBERRY SPRINGS REDUCED VISIBILITY TO NEAR 50 FEET IN A SMALL AREA ON INTERSTATE 40 RESULTING IN AT LEAST 6 BIG RIGS BEING INVOLVED IN AN ACCIDENT. ONE FUEL TRUCK SPILLED AT LEAST 70 GALLONS ONTO THE ROAD.

*** 9 INJ *** BLOWING DUST OFF A DRY LAKE BED NEAR NEWBERRY SPRINGS REDUCED VISIBILITY TO NEAR 50 FEET IN A SMALL AREA ON INTERSTATE 40 RESULTING IN 7 BIG RIGS AND 3 VEHICLES BEING INVOLVED IN A SERIES OF ACCIDENTS. ONE FUEL TRUCK SPILLED AT LEAST 70 GALLONS ONTO THE ROAD. 9 PEOPLE WERE TAKEN TO THE HOSPITAL WITH INJURIES WITH 2 SUFFERING MAJOR INJURIES.
RUNOFF FROM HEAVY RAIN CAUSED SEVERAL INCHES OF FLOWING WATER AND MUD TO CROSS ROADS IN THE MANZANAR NATIONAL HISTORIC SITE. THE AUTO TOUR ROAD WAS CLOSED.
ROCKS AND DEBRIS WASHED ONTO VARIOUS SECTIONS OF WHITNEY PORTAL ROAD FROM WEST OF LONE PINE ALL THE WAY TO PARKING AREA FOR THE TRAIL.

SEVERAL PHOTOS THROUGH SOCIAL MEDIA SHOWING A THIN ROPE TORNADO JUST EAST OF HIGHWAY 95 NEAR THE TURN OFF TO NELSONS LANDING. THE TORNADO TOUCHED THE GROUND FOR ABOUT 30 SECONDS BEFORE LIFTING. ESTIMATED EF0.

NEVADA HIGHWAY PATROL REPORTED FLASH FLOODING ACROSS HIGHWAY 95 NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF HIGHWAY 160 AS WELL AS NEAR MILE MARKERS 18 AND 20 ON HIGHWAY 95. TRAFFIC CONTROL WAS BEING CONDUCTED DUE TO THE FLOODING.

AIR RESOUCES LAB FORECASTER RELAYED A REPORT OF SEVERAL INCHES OF WATER AND DEBRIS CROSSING ROADS ON AREA 6 OF THE NEVADA NATIONAL SECURITY SITE. THE NYE COUNTY SHERIFF WAS CLOSING OFF ROADS ON THE SITE DUE TO THE FLOODING.

AIR RESOUCES LAB FORECASTER RELAYED A REPORT OF SEVERAL INCHES OF WATER AND DEBRIS CROSSING ROADS ON AREA 6 OF THE NEVADA NATIONAL SECURITY SITE. THE NYE COUNTY SHERIFF WAS CLOSING OFF ROADS ON THE SITE DUE TO THE FLOODING. THE M28 AUTOMATED RAIN GAUGE ON THE SITE MEASURED 3.16 INCHES OF RAIN FOR THE DATE ON AUGUST 4TH WHICH SET AN ALL-TIME DAILY RECORD FOR THIS SITE. THE PREVIOUS RECORD WAS 2.10 ON AUGUST 21, 2007 BASED ON RECORDS BACK TO 1997.

AT LEAST 20 HOMES WERE DAMAGED AND MANY PEOPLE HAD TO BE EVACUATED WHEN A MAJOR FLASH FLOOD IMPACTED THE MOAPA AREA BEGINNING AROUND 210 PM SEP 8TH. STATE ROUTE 168 WAS DAMAGED AND CLOSED FOR ABOUT 10 HOURS BETWEEN MOAPA AND COYOTE SPRINGS. AT LEAST 160 STUDENTS HAD TO SHELTER IN PLACE AT THEIR SCHOOLS UNTIL ABOUT 7 PM THAT EVENING.
THE REID GARDNER POWER GENERATING PLANT PARKING LOT WAS FILLED WITH NEARLY 6 FEET OF FLOODWATER.

SEVERAL ROADS ON THE MOAPA PAIUTE TRIBAL RESERVATION WERE FILLED WITH 5 FEET OF SILT. SOME ROADS WERE WASHED OUT AND HOMES AND FIELDS WERE DAMAGED. DAMAGES WERE ESTIMATED AT 250,000 NOT COUNTING DAMAGE TO HOMES.
PARTS OF THE UNION PACIFIC RAILROAD TRACK WERE WASHED OUT.

*** 1 INJ *** A MAJOR FLASH FLOOD IMPACTED I-15 BETWEEN MILE MARKERS 91 AND 93...NEAR GLENDALE AND MOAPA. SUBSTANTIAL DAMAGE OCCURRED TO BOTH LANES CAUSING THE INTERSTATE TO BE CLOSED FOR REPAIRS FOR AT LEAST 3-4 DAYS. MANY CARS WERE SURROUNDED BY FLOOD WATERS AND ONE MINIVAN WAS CARRIED OFF A 10-15 FT DROP INTO THE WASH BELOW WITH TWO OCCUPANTS WHO EVENTUALLY MADE IT OUT SAFELY. A BYSTANDER SUFFERED A BROKEN ARM WHEN HE JUMPED IN TO HELP THE MINIVAN OCCUPANTS.

CHP REPORTS ABOUT 5 FEET OF WATER FLOWING WITH DEBRIS AND MUD OVER THE ROAD WITH TRAFFICE STOPPED ON BOTH SIDES. CHP HAS CLOSED US-95 NORTH OF CA-62 AS OF 804 PM.
MOHAVE COUNTY DEPT OF TRANSPORTATION TWEETED THAT N. STOCKTON HILL ROAD WAS CLOSED FROM PIERCE FERRY ROAD TO VOCK WASH DUE TO FLOODING.
DOLAN SPRINGS FD REPORTED WATER FLOWING THROUGH WASH ACROSS 7TH STREET. ROAD WAS IMPASSABLE AND FIRE TRUCK WAS STRANDED THERE OVERNIGHT.
GENERAL PUBLIC POSTED A VIDEO AND PICTURES OF WATER RUNNING RAPIDLY THROUGH A WASH OVER LONDON BRIDGE ROAD. ROAD WAS IMPASSABLE.

20 MULE TEAM CANYON ROAD IN DEATH VALLEY NATIONAL PARK WAS CLOSED DUE TO DAMAGE FROM FLASH FLOODING. SALT CREEK ROAD AND TITUS CANYON ROAD ALSO HAD DAMAGE FROM FLASH FLOODING. THE EVENT TIME WAS ESTIMATED.
THUNDERSTORM WINDS KNOCKED OVER A TREE ONTO SUNLAND ROAD BLOCKING ONE LANE OF THE ROAD.
PIPE SPRING NATIONAL MONUMENT STAFF REPORTED HAIL UP TO QUARTER SIZE. EVENT TIME ESTIMATED OFF OF RADAR.
TRAINED SPOTTER MEASURED 1.67 INCHES OF RAIN AT HIS RESIDENCE, MOST OF WHICH FELL DURING A 45 MINUTE PERIOD. HE ALSO REPORTED THAT WATER AS OVER WARM SPRINGS ROAD, MAKING IT IMPASSABLE.
PUBLIC REPORTS WATER AND GOOD SIZED ROCKS FLOWING ACROSS HIGHWAY 93...6 MILES SOUTH OF COYOTE SPRINGS. WATER WAS ALSO STARTING TO COME ACROSS THE ROAD ONE-HALF MILE NORTH OF COYOTE SPRINGS.
TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTS SEVERAL AREAS WITH 6-8 INCHES OF WATER FLOWING ACROSS ROADWAYS IN BOULDER CITY. WATER WAS STILL FLOWING AT 602 PM.
THE ASOS AT THE NORTH LAS VEGAS AIRPORT RECORDED A PEAK WIND GUST OF 63 MPH ASSOCIATED WITH A THUNDERSTORM.
THUNDERSTORM WINDS CAUSED A HEAVY CONSTRUCTION BARRICADE TO BLOW INTO A CAR CAUSING SIGNIFICANT BODY DAMAGE.
BROADCAST MEDIA REPORTS SEVERAL DRIVERS STRANDED IN FLOODWATERS AT N RANCHO DR AND W ALEXANDER RD. WATER WAS REPORTED TO BE AS DEEP AS 1-2 FEET IN SPOTS.
PIOCHE SHERIFF REPORTED THAT US 93 BETWEEN MILE MARKERS 65 AND 70 IS FLOODED. WATER CAME UP OVER THE CULVERTS AND FLOODED THAT SECTION OF US 93. TIME ESTIMATED OFF RADAR.
PUBLIC TWEETS LARGE TREE UPROOTED IN FRONT YARD. THIS OCCURED ROUGHLY 4 MILES NORTH OF N LAS VEGAS AIRPORT, WHICH EARLIER RECORDED A 63 MPH THUNDERSTORM WIND GUST

SEVERAL TREE LIMBS 2-3 INCHES IN DIAMETER WERE BROKEN OFF SEVERAL TREES. THE DOOR OF A BUSINESS WAS COMPLETELY BLOWN IN DUE TO THE WINDS. THIS LOCATION IS APPROXIMATELY 2 MILES AWAY FROM THE NORTH LAS VEGAS AIRPORT WHICH RECORDED 63 MPH FROM EARLIER STORMS.
